# Executive action-proposal PDF, chapter map (BTG master)

Format: ms-identity editorial (A4 portrait, WeasyPrint, Inter + JetBrains Mono, MS 4-color). 12 pages. Locale: client language, PT-BR default for Brazil.

## Page map

1. Cover: eyebrow "GitHub Copilot · Usage-Based Billing · {Client}", title with one yellow em accent line, 60-90 word lede, "Confidencial · {Client}" in cover-top (never "Internal"), audited-base line, author block, 4-color bar.
2. Masthead + TOC: 8 chapters + appendix; sidebars "Como ler" and "A tese, em uma linha".
3. Ch01 Executive lede (tinted): 3 paragraphs, pull quote reframing adoption growth as the asset, 4 data cards (today / with promo / no action / jump).
4. Ch02 What changed: seat price unchanged, opaque cap to visible meter, pooling benefit quantified; 3 callouts (UNCHANGED / NEW / PROTECTION) + sidebar "why the bill grew".
5. Ch03 The three scenarios (tinted): line chart no-action vs with-plan (chart_line, fill on red series), the jump, the FY delta; reading-the-chart sidebar.
6. Ch04 Where the cost lives: model mix h-bar with GENERIC class labels (frontier A, premium B...) in the client doc, full model names stay in the XLSX; cache-read share callout; sidebar explaining the generic names.
7. Ch05 Commercial track: P3 explained, criterion = beat the current ACD (declared), window before promo expiry; "what we do not promise" sidebar. NEVER write internal mechanism names like "Deal Desk"; say "condicoes acima da tabela publicada, sujeitas a elegibilidade e aprovacao" (with accents in real copy).
8. Ch06 Technical track: R1-R7 grouped callouts (R1+R2 / R4+R5 / R6+R7), lean 20-30% vs mature 55-70% bands with sources, hero number = FY redemption.
9. Ch07 6-month plan (tinted): three 30-day-window callouts, biweekly cadence, September checkpoint sidebar with the date.
10. Ch08 Next steps: what we ask (3 names, 1 access, 1 date) / what Microsoft delivers; week-by-week bullets; closing pull quote.
11. Appendix: scenario table (monthly/annual/note) + Sources sidebar + Declared assumptions sidebar (consumption held flat = conservative; lean band; commercial conditions subject to approval).
12. Dark close: MS + GitHub marks, thesis quote, "Fim do documento".

## Client-language rules

- Tone: decision document, not sales. Numbers first, bands with sources, criteria declared.
- Forbidden in client docs: internal mechanism names, objection-bank phrasing, competitor disparagement, guaranteed discounts.
- Keep the adoption-growth reframe: growth is the asset; the plan protects budget without slowing the team.

## PT-BR accent checklist (delivery blocker if failed)

Write copy with full accents from the start. Before export, scan the rendered PDF text for these ASCII forms; any hit means broken copy: " acao", " nao ", "promocao", "cenario", "transicao", "governanca", "orcamento", "credito", "grafico", "licencas", " mes ", "tres ". Bulk ASCII-to-accent replacement is error-prone (substring traps like pos to pós breaking "proposta"); prefer writing it right the first time.
